title: Indian Miniature Vision
definition: A unique visual style in classical Indian painting, characterized by a flat, ornate surface and figures depicted from the side, rather than with Renaissance perspective.

difficulty_levels.beginner: Indian miniatures have a distinctive look where people are often drawn from the side and the use of 3D perspective is minimal.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: Classical Indian painting uses a different visual language than Post-Renaissance European art, as evidenced by its lack of traditional linear perspective.
difficulty_levels.expert: The Indian miniature vision represents a distinct cultural approach to representing space and self, which diverges significantly from the European Renaissance tradition.

extra_fields.insight: Artistic traditions can reveal deeper insights into the collective ways of seeing within a culture.
